description:
Collect and process observability data at the edge

Cribl Edge helps you collect and process observability data - logs, metrics, and application data - in real time, from your Linux, Windows, and macOS machines, apps, or microservices, and deliver them to Cribl Stream or any supported destination.
With Cribl Edge you can:
- Automatically discover and collect host, container, and application metrics, as well as logs, on endpoints.
- Process data with Functions and Pipelines.
- Forward data to any of the many supported destinations.
- Easily manage configurations while maintaining version control.
- Teleport to the edge for local preview and configuration validation.
This release corrects a critical issue found in Cribl Edge 4.18.0:
- Enabling Source persistent queue (PQ) in Smart mode caused the __inputId field to be set to <sourcetype>:default (for example, syslog:default) instead of the correct source ID. This broke route filters that matched on __inputId, causing events to miss their intended routes. Affected Syslog and Splunk TCP sources. For more information, see the known issue. (CRIBL-41322)
See the Cribl Edge 4.18.0 release notes for the latest feature and correction updates.
